conversations.content: I found this on the trail — what is it?
Good eye — this is Roundleaf Henbane. Hyoscyamus albus, the white henbane or yellow henbane, is a plant in the family of Solanaceae.
What plant is this?
That looks like Acacia Petit Feuille. Acacia confusa is a perennial tree native to South-East Asia.

That's Aceitillo. Pilocarpus is a genus of about 13 species of plants belonging to the family Rutaceae, native to the Neotropics of South America.
Do you know what kind of plant this is?
This is Fireweed. Chamaenerion latifolium is a species of flowering plant in the evening primrose family known by the English common names dwarf fireweed and river beauty willowherb.
What species is this plant?
You've spotted Meadow Thistle. Cirsium dissectum, also known as meadow thistle, is an erect perennial herb.

Good eye — this is Whorled Stonecrop. Sedum ternatum is the most widespread native Sedum species in eastern North America, commonly known as woodland stonecrop.
Can you identify this species?
This appears to be Coastal Stonecrop. Sedum litoreum is a species of succulent annual herb in the family Crassulaceae.
What plant is this?
Good eye — this is Garden Lettuce. Lettuce is an annual plant of the family Asteraceae mostly grown as a leaf vegetable.
I saw this growing near the trail. Any idea what it is?
That looks like Common Lavender. Lavandula angustifolia, formerly L. officinalis, is a flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae, native to the Mediterranean basin.
I found this on the trail — what is it?
That's Hedge-leaved Adenostyle. Adenostyles alliariae is herbaceous perennial flowering plant in the daisy family Asteraceae.

That's 7-weeks-fern. Rumohra adiantiformis, the leather fern or leatherleaf fern, is a species of fern in the wood fern family Dryopteridaceae.
What species is this plant?
Looks like Foliage-flower to me. Breynia disticha, commonly known as the snowbush, is a plant in the family Phyllanthaceae, first described in 1776.
What's the name of this plant?
You've spotted Spotted Peperomia. Peperomia maculosa, commonly known as spotted-stalked peperomia and spotted peperomia, is a species of plant in the genus Peperomia.
What species is this plant?

This appears to be Burflower-tree. Neolamarckia cadamba, with English common names burflower-tree, laran, and Leichhardt pine, and called kadamba or kadam or cadamba locally, is an evergreen, tropical tree native to South and Southeast Asia.
What species is this plant?

This is Birdflower. Crotalaria laburnifolia, called the rattle pod, is a highly variable species of flowering plant in the genus Crotalaria, widely distributed in central, eastern and southern Africa, the Seychelles, the Indian Subcontinent, Thailand, the Lesser Sunda Islands, Java, and Queensland in Australia.
What plant is this?
This is Blue Wattle. Acacia dealbata, commonly known as silver wattle, blue wattle or mimosa, is a species of flowering plant in the legume family, Fabaceae.
Describe this plant.
You're looking at Early Winter-Cress. Barbarea intermedia is a species of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae.
Can you identify this species?
That looks like Heart Lettuce. Lactuca saligna is a species of wild lettuce known by the common name willowleaf lettuce, and least lettuce.
Help me identify this — is it a common species?

This appears to be Himalayan Berry. Sea-buckthorn, also known as sea buckthorn, sandthorn, sallowthorn or seaberry, is a species of flowering plant in the family Elaeagnaceae, native to cold-temperate regions of Eurasia.
What plant is this?
That looks like Bur Grass. Cenchrus echinatus is a species of grass known by the common names southern sandbur, spiny sandbur, southern sandspur, and in Australia, Mossman River grass.
What's the name of this plant?
This appears to be Buttercup Family. herb,vine

This is Dark Red Helleborine. Epipactis atrorubens, the dark-red helleborine or royal helleborine, is an herbaceous plant in the orchid family, Orchidaceae.

That's Pink Stonecrop. Sedum cepaea, the pink stonecrop, is a species of flowering plant in the family Crassulaceae.

Can you identify this species?
That looks like Heartleaf Keckiella. Keckiella cordifolia is a species of flowering shrub in the plantain family known by the common name heartleaf keckiella.
What species is this plant?
Looks like Chilean Bellflower to me. Lapageria is a genus of flowering plants with only one known species, Lapageria rosea, commonly known as Chilean bellflower or copihue.
I found this on the trail — what is it?
Good eye — this is Dwarf Erycina. Erycina pusilla is a species of flowering plants, which is a tiny orchid with an overall size of 2.5 to 3.5 cm from the orchid family, Orchidaceae.
Describe this plant.
You're looking at Apetalous Sandwort. Moehringia trinervia, commonly known as apetalous sandwort or three-nerved sandwort, is a herbaceous flowering plant in the family Caryophyllaceae.
Can you identify this species?
That's American Angelica-tree. Aralia spinosa, commonly known as devil's walking stick, is a woody species of plant in the genus Aralia of the family Araliaceae.

Looks like Screw-bean to me. Strombocarpa pubescens, commonly known as screwbean mesquite, is a species of flowering shrub or small tree in the pea family, Fabaceae, that is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico.
What's the name of this plant?
Looks like Grape-leaved Anemone to me. Eriocapitella vitifolia, a species of flowering plant in the buttercup family Ranunculaceae, is native to Asia.
What species is this plant?

You've spotted Raffia Palm. Raphia farinifera is a tropical African palm tree occurring in lowland riparian and swamp forest, also around human habitations and cultivated locations, on stream banks and other moist situations at altitudes of 50–1000 m.
I'm curious about this plant. What can you tell me?
This is Atlantic Giant Pumpkin. Cucurbita maxima, one of at least five species of cultivated squash, is one of the most diverse domesticated species.
I found this on the trail — what is it?
You're looking at Bee Orchid. Ophrys apifera, known in Europe as the bee orchid, is a perennial herbaceous plant of the genus Ophrys, in the family of Orchidaceae.
What am I looking at?
This is Blue Lupine. Lupinus pilosus, commonly known as blue lupine, is a species of flowering plant that is edible from the family Fabaceae that grows in Mediterranean scrubland.
What species is this plant?

This appears to be Black Thistle. Cirsium vulgare, commonly known as spear thistle, bull thistle, or common thistle, is a species of the Asteraceae genus Cirsium, native throughout most of Europe, Western Asia, and northwestern Africa.
What's the name of this plant?
Looks like Gold Medallion to me. Melampodium divaricatum, also known by its common name gold medallion is a species of flowering plant from the genus Melampodium.
I found this on the trail — what is it?
You've spotted Narcissus Anemone. Anemonastrum narcissiflorum, the narcissus anemone or narcissus-flowered anemone, is a herbaceous perennial in the genus Anemonastrum and the buttercup family.
I found this on the trail — what is it?
You've spotted Mexican Viper. Maurandya barclayana, commonly called angels trumpet or Mexican viper, is an ornamental plant in the family Plantaginaceae native to Mexico.
I'm curious about this plant. What can you tell me?
That's Annual Canarygrass. Canary grass is a plant, Phalaris canariensis, belonging to the family Poaceae.

You've spotted Hubei Anemone. Eriocapitella hupehensis, a species of flowering plant in the Ranunculaceae family of plants and has an extensive native distribution across a large area of Asia.

This is Kiawe. Neltuma pallida is a species of mesquite tree.
Do you know what kind of plant this is?
Looks like American-sarsaparilla to me. Aralia nudicaulis is a species of flowering plant in the ivy family Araliaceae.
What species is this plant?

This is Alpine Buckler Fern. Dryopteris expansa, the alpine buckler fern, northern buckler-fern or spreading wood fern, is a species of perennial fern native to cool temperate and subarctic regions of the Northern Hemisphere, south at high altitudes in mountains to Spain and Greece in southern Europe, to Japan in eastern Asia, and to central California in North America.
What species is this plant?
That's Broad-leaved Chervil. Chaerophyllum aromaticum is a species of flowering plant belonging to the family Apiaceae.
What's the name of this plant?

That looks like American Joint-vetch. Aeschynomene americana is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae (legume) known by many common names, including shyleaf, forage aeschynomene, American joint vetch, thornless mimosa, bastard sensitive plant (Jamaica), pega pega, pega ropa, antejuela, ronte, cujicillo, and dormilonga.
Describe this plant.

That looks like Oak Ridge Lupine. Lupinus diffusus, commonly known as Oak Ridge lupine, spreading lupine, or sky-blue lupine, is a species of lupine native to the southeastern United States, from North Carolina south to Florida and west to Mississippi.
Do you know what kind of plant this is?
You're looking at Berseem Clover. Trifolium alexandrinum is a species of clover.
What am I looking at?

Good eye — this is Biting Stonecrop. Sedum acre, commonly known as the goldmoss stonecrop, mossy stonecrop, goldmoss sedum, biting stonecrop, and wallpepper, is a perennial flowering plant in the family Crassulaceae.
What's the name of this plant?
That looks like Asian Pumpkin. Cucurbita ficifolia is a species of squash, grown for its edible seeds, fruit, and greens.
Can you identify this species?
This is Bois Patte Poule Piquant. Vepris lanceolata is a large, evergreen tree native to southern and eastern Africa.

That's Egyptian Lily. Nymphaea lotus, the white Egyptian lotus, tiger lotus, white lotus, or Egyptian water-lily, is a flowering plant of the family Nymphaeaceae.

Looks like Clammy Campion to me. Viscaria vulgaris, the sticky catchfly or clammy campion, is a flowering plant in the family Caryophyllaceae.

Looks like African Marigold to me. Tagetes erecta, the Aztec marigold, Mexican marigold, big marigold, cempaxochitl or cempasúchil, is a species of flowering plant in the genus Tagetes native to Mexico and Central America.
Describe this plant.
That's Cutleaf Water Parsnip. Berula erecta, known as lesser water-parsnip, cutleaf waterparsnip, or narrow-leaved water-parsnip, is a member of the carrot family.
I saw this growing near the trail. Any idea what it is?
That looks like Adonis Abbot. Leonotis nepetifolia,, is a species of plant in the genus Leonotis and the family Lamiaceae (mint), native to tropical Africa and the Indian Subcontinent.

This appears to be Bald-Money. Meum is a monotypic genus in the family Apiaceae.

Good eye — this is Lenga Beech. Nothofagus pumilio, the lenga beech, is a deciduous tree or shrub in the Nothofagaceae family that is native to the southern Andes range, in the temperate forests of Chile and Argentina to Tierra del Fuego, from 35° to 56° South latitude.
What am I looking at?
Good eye — this is Lance-leaf Stonecrop. Sedum lanceolatum is a species of flowering plant in the stonecrop family known by the common names lanceleaf stonecrop and spearleaf stonecrop.
What species is this plant?
That's Coastal Arrowhead. Sagittaria graminea, the grassy arrowhead or grass-leaved arrowhead, is an aquatic plant species native to eastern North America.
Can you tell me about this plant I just spotted?
This is Arrow-leaved Lettuce. Lactuca canadensis is a species of wild lettuce known by the common names Canada lettuce, Canada wild lettuce, and tall lettuce.

This appears to be Bahama Dildo. Pilosocereus brevispinus is a species of flowering plant in the cactus family Cactaceae, native to central Hispaniola, occurring in both the Dominican Republic and Haiti.
Do you know what kind of plant this is?
This appears to be Pomegranate. The pomegranate is a fruit-bearing, deciduous shrub in the family Lythraceae, subfamily Punicoideae, that grows to between 1.5–5 metres (5–16 ft) tall.
I saw this growing near the trail. Any idea what it is?
Looks like Hexagon Stonecrop to me. Sedum sexangulare, also known as tasteless stonecrop, is a species of succulent perennial and evergreen plant of the genus Sedum.

This is Alexanders. Angelica atropurpurea, known commonly as purplestem angelica, great angelica, American angelica, high angelica, and masterwort, is a species of flowering plant that can be found in moist and swampy woodlands, mostly by riverbanks, in eastern North America.
Describe this plant.
This is Mueller's Epipactis. Epipactis muelleri is a species of orchid.
Describe this plant.
This appears to be Mosaic-plant. Fittonia albivenis is a species of flowering plant in the family Acanthaceae, native to the rainforests of Colombia, Peru, Bolivia, Ecuador and northern Brazil.

That's Meadow Pasqueflower. Pulsatilla pratensis, the small pasque flower, is a species of flowering plant in the family Ranunculaceae, native to central and eastern Europe, from southeast Norway and western Denmark south and east to Bulgaria.

You're looking at Common Nipplewort. Lapsana communis, the common nipplewort, is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae.
Describe this plant.

This appears to be Flax-leaved Daphne. Daphne gnidium, the flax-leaved daphne, is a poisonous evergreen shrub from the northern and western Mediterranean region, with narrow, dense dark-green foliage and white fragrant flowers.

That looks like Cone Cactus. Cephalocereus polylophus is a species of cactus endemic to Mexico.

Good eye — this is Balloon Cotton Bush. Gomphocarpus physocarpus, commonly known as hairy balls, balloonplant, balloon cotton-bush, bishop's balls, nailhead, or swan plant, is a species of plant in the Milkweed subfamily Asclepiadoideae of the Dogbane Family (Apocynaceae), related to the milkweeds.
Can you tell me about this plant I just spotted?
You're looking at Balsam-scented Geranium. Holocacista varii is a moth of the family Heliozelidae.

This appears to be California Spicebush. Calycanthus, called sweetshrub, is a genus of flowering plants in the family Calycanthaceae.

Describe this plant.

Looks like Haller's Anemone to me. Pulsatilla halleri, synonym Anemone halleri, Haller's anemone, is a species of flowering plant in the buttercup family Ranunculaceae, that can be found in sub-alpine and alpine regions of southern France, southern Switzerland, northern Italy and Poland, extending eastward to Greece, Bulgaria and Ukraine.

That's Hay-scent Buckler Fern. Dryopteris aemula, the hay-scented buckler-fern or hay-scented fern, is a species of perennial leptosporangiate fern.

That looks like Ethiopian Rattlebox. Crotalaria is a genus of flowering plants in the family Fabaceae commonly known as rattlepods.

This is American Carrot. Daucus pusillus is a species of wild carrot known by the common names American wild carrot and rattle-snake-weed.
